LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY, DEUTSCHE BANK AG, AND NICOLE DESANTIS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, AND THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. THE DEMURRER ON THE GROUNDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S USE OF A PSEUDONYM IS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. THE DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR QUANTUM MERUIT IS OVERRULED. THE QUANTUM MERUIT CAUSE OF ACTION IS NOT BARRED BY THE CONTRACT CAUSE OF ACTION OR THE ARGUMENT THAT DAMAGES ARE DETERMINABLE UNDER THE CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF IS PERMITTED TO PLEAD IN THE ALTERNATIVE. THE DEMURRER TO THE EIGHTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION ON STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS GROUNDS IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY PLED APPLICATION OF THE DISCOVERY RULE AT 64 AND 74 OF THE COMPLAINT. (SEE FOX V. ETHICON ENDO-SURGERY, INC. (2005) 35 CAL.4TH 797, 806..) THE DEMURRER TO THE EIGHTH AND NINTH CAUSES OF ACTION ON PARTICULARITY GROUNDS IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD HOW THE REPRESENTATIONS WERE MADE AND BY WHOM, INCLUDING THEIR AUTHORITY TO SPEAK ON BEHALF OF THE DEFENDANT CORPORATION. (SEE TARMANN V. STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTO. INS., (1991) 2 CAL.APP.4TH 153, 157.) THE DEMURRER TO THE TENTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AND THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED ON ALL GROUNDS. THE CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUFFICIENTLY PLED AND IT CANNOT BE DETERMINED FROM THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT THAT THE ALLEGED DEFAMATORY STATEMENTS ARE NON-ACTIONABLE OPINION OR BARRED BY THE COMMON INTEREST PRIVILEGE FOUND AT CIVIL CODE SECTION 47(C). JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)

The motion to quash is DENIED with respect to Document Requests 2, 3, 5, 7, 8, 9, 10, and 12. Wells Fargo shall produce documents responsive to these requests. Wells Fargo shall also produce documents responsive to Requests 1, 6, and 11 modified as follows: Request 1: "Any applications, resumes, cover letters, business plans or similar DOCUMENTS of Deutsche Bank obtained by YOU or provided to YOU by PLAINTIFF in anticipation of PLAINTIFF's employment or prospective employment with YOU." Request No. 6: "Contents of PLAINTIFF's personnel file that reflect PLAINTIFF's date of hire, PLAINTIFF'S earnings, and/or PLAINTIFF's reason(s) for leaving Deutsche Bank (as well as any contents that are responsive to another Document Request)." Request No. 11. "Any human resources records RELATING TO request(s) made by PLAINTIFF or her medical providers for reasonable accommodation or other job modification(s) based on any mental or emotional condition of PLAINTIFF during her employment with YOU. The parties shall also prepare a stipulated protective order governing the use of any documents produced by the parties or third-parties in this litigation. The parties' requests for sanctions are DENIED.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. S. ADAM SPIEWAK OF THE EMILY LEVIN LAW CORPORATION, TEL. NO. (213) 537-5291 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. TIMOTHY M. RUSCHE OF SEYFARTH & SHAW LLP, TEL. NO. (213) 270-9600 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY and DEUTSCHE BANK AG, NICOLE DESANTIS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled in part and sustained in part with leave to amend, and the motion to strike is denied. The demurrer on the grounds that Plaintiff's use of a pseudonym is taken under submission. The demurrer to the fifth cause of action for quantum meruit is overruled. The quantum meruit cause of action is not barred by the contract cause of action or the argument that damages are determinable under the contract. Plaintiff is permitted to plead in the alternative. The demurrer to the eighth and ninth causes of action on statute of limitations grounds is overruled. Plaintiff has sufficiently pled application of the discovery rule at 64 and 74 of the Complaint. (See Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 806..) The demurrer to the eighth and ninth causes of action on particularity grounds is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead how the representations were made and by whom, including their authority to speak on behalf of the defendant corporation. (See Tarmann v. State Farm Mutual Auto. Ins., (1991) 2 Cal.App.4th 153, 157.) The demurrer to the tenth cause of action is overruled and the motion to strike is denied on all grounds. The cause of action is sufficiently pled and it cannot be determined from the face of the complaint that the alleged defamatory statements are non-actionable opinion or barred by the common interest privilege found at Civil Code section 47(c). Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)
